title: [VPlan] Create edge mask for single-destination switch
author: Noratrieb
description:
When converting phis to blends, the `VPPredicator` expects to have edge masks to the phi node if the phi node has different incoming blocks. This was not the case if the predecessor of the phi was a switch where a conditional destination was the same as the default destination.

This was because when creating edge masks in `createSwitchEdgeMasks`, edge masks are set in a loop through the *non-default* destinations. But when there are no non-default destinations (but at least one condition, otherwise an earlier condition would trigger and just forward the source mask), this loop is never executed, so the masks are never set.

To resolve this, we explicitly forward the source mask for these cases as well, which is correct because it is an unconditional branch, just a very convoluted one.

fixes #179074

tests.test_body:
define void @switch_unconditional_duplicate_target(ptr %start, ptr %dest) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %latch ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %start, i64 %iv
  %x = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %cond = icmp ult i32 %x, 10
  br i1 %cond, label %forward, label %latch

forward:                                          ; preds = %loop
  switch i32 %x, label %latch [
    i32 0, label %latch
  ]

latch:                                            ; preds = %forward, %forward, %loop
  %gep.1 = phi ptr [ %gep, %loop ], [ %dest, %forward ], [ %dest, %forward ]
  store i32 0, ptr %gep.1, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %latch
  ret void
}

define void @switch_unconditional(ptr %start) {
entry:
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop.latch ]
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %start, i64 %iv
  %x = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  switch i32 %x, label %foo [
  ]

foo:                                              ; preds = %loop.header
  br label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %foo
  store i32 0, ptr %gep, align 4
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %loop.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch
  ret void
}

comments.author: Noratrieb
comments.body: I confirmed that cherry-picking this into Rust main's current LLVM 22 fixes the originally reported rustc crash.

comments.author: Noratrieb
comments.body: Also cc @nikic as this has been observed to cause crashes in rustc (#151926) after you updated rustc to LLVM 22 (I guess because some other pass now generates this IR pattern?), so I think it would make sense to ensure this gets into LLVM 22.

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: @Noratrieb do you need me to land this change for you? The cherry-picking for the release is driven via the issue once the fix has landed
